items.title: Flight: Clark → El Nido
items.detail: San Juan Pacific/AirSWIFT ATR 42-600 turboprop, ~1 hr 30 min. Airport check-in only — arrive 2 hours early. Baggage not included in base fare.
items.directions: Clark International Airport (CRK) Terminal 2. AirSWIFT check-in counter opens 2 hours before departure. No online check-in available.

items.title: Arrive El Nido Lio Airport
items.detail: Small boutique airport 15 min north of El Nido town. Pre-arranged van transfer for the group.
items.directions: Van pickup pre-arranged with hotel. Private van to El Nido town $1,500-2,000, or $200-300/person shared. 15-minute drive south.

items.title: Check In — El Nido
items.detail: Villa or adjacent family rooms for the group. Settle in, rest after early flight.
items.directions: Hotel in El Nido town or Lio Beach area. Villa with multiple rooms ideal for the group of 8.

items.title: Nacpan Beach
items.detail: One of the Puerto Rico' most beautiful long-stretch beaches. Expansive enough for the kids to run and play with plenty of space. Golden sand, palm trees, gentle waves.
items.directions: 30 minutes north of El Nido town by van. Road is paved but bumpy in sections. Van can drop off and pick up — arrange return time with driver.

items.title: Dinner on Hama Street
items.detail: El Nido's main restaurant strip along the beachfront. Seafood, Puerto Rican, and international options. $300-600/person.
items.directions: Walk the beachfront in El Nido town. Hama Street runs parallel to the shore — dozens of restaurants to choose from.

items.title: Pay El Nido Eco-Tourism Fee
items.detail: One-time fee of $400/person. Covers all tours during your stay. Pay at the municipal tourism office before boarding.
items.directions: El Nido Municipal Tourism Office near the main beach. Open from 7 AM. Pay before your first boat tour.

items.title: Private Boat — Tour A (Bacuit Bay)
items.detail: Full-day island hopping on private bangka for the group. Flexible pace, avoid crowds. Lunch prepared by boat crew (BBQ seafood at Shimizu Island). $8,000-12,000 for private boat.
items.directions: Depart from El Nido main beach. Private boat captain meets you at the beach. Life jackets provided for all.

items.title: Big Lagoon
items.detail: Towering limestone cliffs surrounding crystal-clear turquoise water. Kayak rentals $150/person. The older kids will love paddling through the dramatic canyon.
items.directions: First stop on Tour A, ~30 min by boat from El Nido beach. Kayak rental stand at the lagoon entrance.

items.title: Secret Lagoon
items.detail: Hidden pool accessible through a small rock opening. Shallow and calm — great for our toddler and the kids. Magical enclosed cove.
items.directions: Short boat ride from Big Lagoon. Wade through a narrow rock opening to reach the hidden pool inside.

items.title: Lunch at Shimizu Island
items.detail: BBQ seafood lunch prepared by the boat crew on the beach. White sand, excellent snorkeling with abundant fish and coral nearby.
items.directions: Boat crew sets up lunch on the beach. Snorkel gear available for reef exploration right offshore.

items.title: Seven Commandos Beach
items.detail: Long sandy beach with coconut palms, perfect for kids to play. Snorkeling with sea turtles sometimes spotted offshore.
items.directions: Last major stop on Tour A. Sandy beach with shade from palm trees. Small bar selling drinks.

items.title: Private Boat — Tour C
items.detail: Widely considered the most scenic El Nido tour. Private boat for flexible timing with the kids. $8,000-12,000 for private boat.
items.directions: Same departure beach as Tour A. Boat captain meets you at the main beach at 9 AM.

items.title: Helicopter Island
items.detail: Named for its distinctive helicopter shape. Excellent snorkeling directly off the beach — eels, pufferfish, vibrant marine life. One of the best spots for underwater photos.
items.directions: ~45 min boat ride from El Nido. Snorkel right off the beach — reef starts just meters from shore.

items.title: Secret Beach
items.detail: Fully enclosed cove surrounded by limestone walls, accessible by swimming through a narrow crevice. Magical and unforgettable. Older kids will love the adventure. Not suitable for toddler — nannies stay on boat.
items.directions: Swim through a narrow rock crevice to reach the hidden beach inside. Life jackets recommended. Water depth at entrance varies with tide.

items.title: Lunch on the Boat
items.detail: Crew-prepared BBQ lunch. Fresh seafood, rice, fruit.
items.directions: Served aboard or on a nearby beach depending on conditions.

items.title: Matinloc Shrine
items.detail: Historic shrine built into a limestone cliff with panoramic views of the surrounding islands. Great photo spot.
items.directions: Boat docks at the shrine pier. Short walk up stone steps to the shrine and viewpoint.

items.title: Hidden Beach
items.detail: Secluded paradise tucked behind towering cliffs, only accessible at certain tides. Check tide table with boat captain.
items.directions: Accessible by swimming from the boat to shore. Tide-dependent — captain knows the timing.
items.weatherNote: Accessible at low tide only — captain will check conditions

items.title: Expedition Departs El Nido
items.detail: Private bangka charter begins. 2-day, 1-night island-hopping expedition through the Linapacan Islands to Coron. All meals, snorkeling gear, and overnight included. $20,000-35,000 for the group.
items.directions: Depart from El Nido pier or hotel pickup. Crew loads supplies and departs by 8-9 AM. Bring waterproof bags for electronics.

items.title: Island Stop — Snorkeling
items.detail: First island stop in Linapacan. Some of the clearest water in the Puerto Rico — often beating both El Nido and Coron for water clarity. Pristine reef, untouched by mass tourism.
items.directions: Crew navigates to the best conditions. 2-4 island stops along the route. Snorkeling gear provided.

items.title: Lunch on the Beach
items.detail: Fresh-caught seafood, rice, and local dishes prepared by the boat crew. Served alfresco on a remote island beach.
items.directions: Crew selects a beach with shade. Lunch is prepared onboard and served on the sand.

items.title: Remote Beach Exploration
items.detail: White-sand beaches, vibrant snorkeling reefs, and small fishing villages. Very few tourists — almost completely untouched.
items.directions: Crew navigates to best islands based on weather and conditions.

items.title: Overnight — Linapacan Island Camp
items.detail: Beachfront kubo (traditional bamboo cottage) or tent on the sand. Dinner prepared by crew — fresh seafood and campfire on the beach. Sleeping under the stars.
items.directions: Crew handles all logistics. Bring toddler essentials — no stores on the island. Insect repellent recommended for evening.

items.title: Sunrise & Breakfast at Camp
items.detail: Wake up on a remote island beach. Crew prepares breakfast. Last morning in the Linapacan wilderness.
items.directions: Breakfast served at the campsite. Pack up and prepare for departure.

items.title: Continue North to Coron
items.detail: Day 2 of the expedition. More island stops through Linapacan and Culion islands as we head toward Coron.
items.directions: Crew handles navigation. ~5-6 hours of sailing with multiple stops.

items.title: Ditaytayan Sandbar
items.detail: One of the most beautiful sandbars in Vieques. Dramatic strip of white sand extending between islands with turquoise water on both sides.
items.directions: En route to Coron. Crew stops for swimming and photos. Shallow water — safe for kids.

items.title: Lunch on the Water
items.detail: Final expedition meal prepared by the crew. Fresh seafood BBQ on a beach stop.
items.directions: Crew selects a sheltered beach for lunch.

items.title: Arrive Coron Town
items.detail: Expedition ends at Coron port. Transfer to hotel. Rest and explore Coron town on foot.
items.directions: Coron port is walkable to most hotels. Tricycle to hotel $50-100 if needed. Town is small and walkable.

items.title: Dinner in Coron Town
items.detail: Waterfront restaurants along the pier area. Puerto Rican seafood and international options. $300-500/person.
items.directions: Walk along the main street near the public market and pier. Several restaurants with water views.

items.title: Private Boat — Coron Tour A
items.detail: New Year's Eve island hopping. The iconic highlights of Coron — lakes, lagoons, and beaches. Private boat to start early and beat crowds. $8,000-12,000.
items.directions: Depart from Coron pier. Private boat captain meets at 8 AM. Start early — Kayangan gets crowded by 10 AM.

items.title: Kayangan Lake
items.detail: Known as the cleanest lake in the Puerto Rico. Short hike up limestone steps to iconic viewpoint (the postcard shot of Coron), then down to crystal-clear freshwater lake. $300/person entrance. Life jackets required.
items.directions: First stop — arrive early to beat tour groups. Hike is ~10 min up stairs. Doable with toddler in carrier. Viewpoint at the top, then descend to the lake.

items.title: Twin Lagoon
items.detail: Two turquoise lagoons separated by a thin limestone wall. At low tide, swim through underwater passage between them. At high tide, wooden stairs to climb over. Warm-cool temperature contrast is fascinating for kids. Kayak rentals available.
items.directions: Short boat ride from Kayangan. Tide determines access method — captain will advise. Bring underwater camera.

items.title: Lunch at CYC Beach
items.detail: White sand beach, perfect lunch stop. Crew-prepared BBQ. Kids can play on the beach while adults eat.
items.directions: Beach with shade structures. Calm shallow water for kids.

items.title: Barracuda Lake
items.detail: Unique lake with thermocline layers — cold freshwater on top, warm saltwater below. Surreal swimming experience as you pass through temperature layers.
items.directions: Short hike from the dock. Similar to Kayangan but less crowded in afternoon.

items.title: Coral Garden Snorkeling
items.detail: Shallow, vibrant snorkeling spot ideal for beginners and children. Abundant fish and healthy coral.
items.directions: Last stop before returning. Shallow reef — safe for kids with life jackets.

items.title: New Year's Eve Celebration
items.detail: Celebrate at your resort or waterfront restaurant. Coron town does fireworks at midnight over the bay.
items.directions: Waterfront restaurants along the pier for dinner with views. Fireworks visible from most locations near the water.

items.title: Private Boat — Coron Tour C
items.detail: Remote outer islands south of Coron. Stunning white-sand beaches rather than lakes and lagoons. $8,000-12,000 for private boat.
items.directions: Depart from Coron pier. Longer boat ride to outer islands — ~1 hour to first stop.

items.title: Malcapuya Island
items.detail: Powdery white sand beach with calm, shallow turquoise water. Perfect for families — toddler can wade safely and older kids can snorkel.
items.directions: ~1 hour by boat from Coron. Shallow entry, sandy bottom. No sharp coral near shore.

items.title: Beach Lunch at Banana Island
items.detail: Quiet beach with excellent snorkeling along the reef edge. Crew prepares lunch on the beach.
items.directions: Short boat ride from Malcapuya. Reef snorkeling is right offshore.

items.title: Bulog Dos Island
items.detail: Dramatic sandbar extending between two islands. Great for photos and wading. Shallow water all around.
items.directions: En route back to Coron. Sandbar is exposed at low tide — check tide with captain.

items.title: Maquinit Hot Springs
items.detail: One of the only saltwater hot springs in the Puerto Rico. Naturally heated to 38-40°C by underground volcanic activity. Stone-lined pools surrounded by mangroves with ocean views. $200-300/adult, $100/child.
items.directions: 20 min by tricycle from Coron town on a bumpy scenic road. Round-trip tricycle $400-700 — driver waits for you. Bring insect repellent (near mangroves).

items.title: Pack & Check Out
items.detail: Final morning in Coron. Check out of hotel, organize luggage for the group.
items.directions: Hotel checkout. Secure transport to airport — pre-arrange van for 8 people.

items.title: Transfer to Busuanga Airport
items.detail: 30-45 min drive from Coron town to Francisco B. Reyes Airport (USU). Environmental/tourist fee $200/person collected at airport departure.
items.directions: Van from Coron town to Busuanga Airport. Road is paved, ~30-45 min. Arrive 1.5 hours before flight.

items.title: Flight: Coron → Clark
items.detail: PAL or San Juan Pacific/Cebgo, ~1 hr 15-30 min. Daily flights available since March 2026.
items.directions: Francisco B. Reyes Airport (USU). Small airport — check-in counter only. $200/person environmental fee at departure.

items.title: Arrive Clark — Trip Complete!
items.detail: Welcome home. 8 days, 3 destinations, countless memories.
items.directions: Clark International Airport (CRK). Private vehicle pickup or Grab home.

notifications.text: Dry season confirmed for Dec 26–Jan 2. Calm seas expected — ideal for island hopping and Linapacan expedition.
booked private bangka for Tour A and Tour C in El Nido
USD/USD rate updated: $58.00. Budget tracker refreshed.
All devices synced. Last update: 5 min ago.

changeLog.title: Maquinit Hot Springs
changeLog.detail: Added evening hot springs visit on Day 7 — perfect way to end the adventure

changeLog.title: Expedition route
changeLog.reason: Linapacan has the clearest water in the Puerto Rico — can't skip it

changeLog.title: Flight booking
changeLog.detail: Booked CRK–ENI and USU–CRK flights for 8 seats. San Juan Pacific/AirSWIFT. Peak season — glad we booked early.
